_domain_hint: Iran / Israel / Houthi / IRGC theatre. Sources: Reuters, AP, Al Jazeera, BBC, IDF spokesperson, IRGC statements, Iranian state media (IRNA/Tasnim/Mehr/Press TV), UKMTO maritime bulletins, ISW Iran updates.
claim: Iran capitulated to US demands on April 8, 2026, accepting US terms unconditionally.
claim_origin: Pro-US/pro-Israel commentary framing of the ceasefire announcement

sources.text: We received a 10 point proposal from Iran, and believe it is a workable basis on which to negotiate. Almost all of the various points of past contention have been agreed to between the United States and Iran, but a two week period will allow the Agreement to be finalized and consummated.

sources.publisher: Seyed Abbas Araghchi (Iranian Foreign Minister, Supreme National Security Council)
sources.text: If attacks against Iran are halted, our Powerful Armed Forces will cease their defensive operations. [Statement framed as conditional response to Pakistani mediation, not unilateral acceptance of US terms.]
Both parties have displayed remarkable wisdom and understanding and have remained constructively engaged in furthering the cause of peace and stability.

gold.rationale_summary: The agreement structure does not support a 'capitulation' narrative. Trump's own statement references 'a 10 point proposal FROM Iran' that the US accepted as 'workable basis' — Iran set terms, US received them. Araghchi's language frames Iranian action as conditional ('IF attacks against Iran are halted'), maintains language of 'Powerful Armed Forces' and 'defensive operations' — preserving Iranian autonomy framing. Sharif characterizes both parties as having shown 'wisdom and understanding' and being 'constructively engaged' — symmetrical mediator framing. This is a mediated mutual de-escalation through Pakistani channels, not unilateral capitulation.
required_caveats: The agreement structure shows Iranian conditions accepted by the US (10-point Iranian proposal as basis), not vice versa.
Both sides used face-saving language; neither party publicly accepted defeat or surrender framing.
